title: A Multi-Stage Adaptive Cadence Strategy for Heterogeneous Pulsar Populations
description: Proposes a two-stage follow-up system: Stage 1 ('Confirmation and Triage') with 3-4 intensive observations in the first week to secure phase connection, estimate P and P-dot, and probabilistically classify the pulsar. Stage 2 ('Optimized Timing') transitions to a sub-population-specific cadence, e.g., frequent short observations for MSPs, logarithmically spaced longer ones for young pulsars. Uses `psrpoppy` simulations to generate a realistic discovery census and simulate the follow-up pipeline. Success measured by the fraction of the population achieving phase connection within two years; analytic models define failure points, validated against simulations. Addresses initial uncertainty and optimizes resource allocation adaptively.

title: Noise-Centric Cadence Optimization for Extreme and Quiescent Pulsars
description: Designs cadence strategies tailored to the dominant noise source for each pulsar sub-population, arguing that a single cadence is suboptimal. For MSPs: frequent observations to average down radiometer and jitter noise, using a wide band to track DM variations. For young pulsars: less frequent, robust cadence to characterize red noise and detect glitches. For Galactic Center pulsars: high-frequency, rapid-response cadence to combat scattering and DM variability. Simulates TOAs with realistic injected noise, applies various cadences, and uses parameter-fitting to measure P and P-dot recovery precision. Analytic Fisher information calculations predict theoretical best-case precision for validation.

title: A Dichotomous Follow-up Strategy for Targeted versus Blind Surveys
description: Develops two independent follow-up strategies optimized for (1) Targeted Search and (2) Blind Search modes, leveraging prior information from discovery method. 'Targeted' strategy: optimized for MSPs in globular clusters and young pulsars in SNRs, using high S/N from long dwell times for high-cadence or glitch-robust campaigns. 'Blind' strategy: for field pulsars and rare exotica, with short dwell times and lower S/N, prioritizing intensive initial follow-up before transitioning to long-term timing. Uses `psrpoppy` simulations to model yields from each survey type and optimizes total telescope time for each independently. Links real-time search constraints to subsequent timing strategy to maximize scientific yield.

title: Probabilistic Cadence Design for Characterizing Emission-Intermittent Pulsars
description: Focuses on RRATs, nullers, and intermittent pulsars, for which standard cadences are ineffective. Initial 'scouting phase' of dense observations to determine nulling fraction, mean 'on'/'off' durations, and RRAT burst rate. Develops a probabilistic model to predict detection likelihood in future observations based on these parameters. Follow-up cadence is dynamically scheduled to maximize detections for a given telescope time, possibly resulting in non-uniform, clustered observations. Simulates intermittent emitters to test strategy effectiveness in building partial phase-connected solutions. Analytic work derives optimal observation probability as a function of source statistics and telescope sensitivity.

title: Dynamic Cadence Scheduling via Real-Time GPU-Accelerated Analysis
description: Leverages the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL144 backend for real-time analysis and dynamic scheduling of follow-up observations. After each observation, new TOA is added and a timing fit is performed immediately on a GPU; uncertainties in P and P-dot are used to calculate the maximum time until next observation before phase-connection is lost. Telescope scheduler uses this information to optimize the observing queue in real-time, moving from fixed to responsive cadence. Simulates this 'just-in-time' scheduling for a diverse pulsar population and compares efficiency to standard fixed-interval cadences. Project's success depends on backend's ability to keep pace with data flow and enable analytic calculation and re-scheduling within operational timescales.
